text.title: Model Onboarding Checklist — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `model-onboarding-checklist` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Onboarding is launched only by a named, observable trigger (provider announcement URL, internal request ticket, or capability gap). Vague triggers are rejected.
rule.rationale: Trigger-action discipline keeps onboarding off the «cool model dropped» treadmill; explicit triggers make cadence reviewable.

rule.statement: Apply the four phases in order — intake → safety/eval → integration → production-gate. Phase k+1 starts only after phase k gate passes with named approver.
rule.rationale: Skipping phases produces vibes-promotions: routes flipped without evals or rollback plan, which is the #1 cause of incident-worthy regressions.

rule.statement: No traffic share is given to the new model before its golden set + hallucination eval diff vs incumbent is recorded and signed off. Eval-before-route is mandatory.
rule.rationale: Without an eval diff, regressions surface in customer-visible failures rather than in pre-prod measurements.

rule.statement: Per-task cost ($/1k tokens × expected tokens × route share) MUST be projected and compared against incumbent before production exposure.
rule.rationale: Cost surprise is the second-most-common reason model promotions get rolled back; projecting cost in the gate memo makes it explicit and signed.

rule.statement: Each phase gate has a named approver who signs the go/no-go memo. Anonymous «team approved» is rejected.
rule.rationale: RACI discipline: rollback decisions require an accountable owner. Anonymous approvals tend to dissolve at incident time.
